title: Policing and Technology
description: Bringing together debates from law, criminology, socio-legal studies and critical security studies to examine digital governance and policing in postcolonial contexts such as Pakistan.

title: Cybercrime Laws and Enforcement
description: A comparative study of how cybercrime statutes operate across South Asian and Southeast Asian jurisdictions, and what their enforcement means for the societies they govern.

title: Police Pluralisation
description: Collaborative research on security provision by diverse actors, including public, private, hybrid, state and non-state providers, in urban areas of the Global South. Funded by the BA-Leverhulme Small Grant Scheme and UCL's Centre for Global City Policing.

title: Terrains of Disappearances
description: A collaborative project building an international, multidisciplinary network of academics, artists and practitioners examining manifestations of disappearance, loss, grief and absence globally. Led by Professor Nichola Khan with AHRC support.

title: Urban Violence and Insecurity
description: Multiple collaborative projects examining urban violence, insecurity and governance in the Global South through interdisciplinary approaches.
